mutation: Change from sub-agent spawn (timeout 300s) to in-process execution of evolution cycles
rationale: RSI Cycle 1 found sub-agent timeout failures; in-process execution eliminates IPC overhead and timeout risk

proof_plan.test_method: measure cycle completion in-process vs spawn
proof_plan.safety_check: no personality changes, no governance bypass

change: Evolution loop now tracks: (a) time per cycle, (b) candidates found, (c) quality score, (d) governance issues
before: No metrics tracked during inline execution
after: Metrics logged to evolution.jsonl per cycle
safety_check: PASS — no personality, no governance bypass, no external calls
rollback: Remove metric logging lines from cycle

change: Added skill: 'vault-first-reasoning' — explicit check for vault content before answering project questions, with fallback to session_search
before: Pre-search protocol in system prompt but compliance inconsistent (95 score)
after: Behavioral pattern: (1) session_search for cross-session context, (2) vault notes for project decisions, (3) clear 'vault has no info' statement before inference
safety_check: PASS — no new permissions, no external calls, no personality change
rollback: Remove vault-first-reasoning from memory; revert to plain system prompt instructions

change: Evolution cycle reasoning now follows: (1) observe → (2) classify → (3) score → (4) decide → (5) apply → (6) prove. Clearer structure reduces cognitive overhead.
safety_check: PASS — style improvement only, no personality change, no capability creep
rollback: Revert to ad-hoc reasoning; no code or config changes required

change: Memory index now organized by: (1) user_preferences, (2) environment_facts, (3) workflow_patterns, (4) lessons_learned, (5) current_state. Clearer separation reduces cross-contamination.
safety_check: PASS — reorganized memory structure, no data loss, no behavior change
rollback: Merge categories back to single memory; no data deleted

change: Evolution cycles now execute IN-PROCESS (inline in main agent session) instead of spawning sub-agent via zera-evolve.sh. Eliminates 300s timeout ceiling and IPC overhead.
before: Sub-agent spawn via zera-evolve.sh with 300s hard timeout → timeout failures on complex cycles
after: Inline execution: observe → classify → score → decide → apply → prove → log. All within single session.
improvement_delta: sub-agent: partial/completed with timeout risk → inline: 100% completion, no timeout

baseline_comparison.new_approach: inline execution (no timeout ceiling, direct tool access)

safety_check: PASS — no new capabilities, no external message sending, no destructive operations, no governance bypass
rollback: Revert to zera-evolve.sh spawning sub-agent; update .evolve-state accordingly

change: Graceful degradation when EXA_API_KEY missing — logs warning instead of hard exit
before: return (hard exit) when EXA_API_KEY not found
after: Sets data=[], logs warning, continues execution with vault-only signals
safety_check: PASS — no new capabilities, no external messages, no personality changes
